topic: Practical OpenAI and ChatGPT usage patterns, real use cases

entries.q: What is the OpenAI Cookbook and where can I access it
entries.a: The OpenAI Cookbook is a resource for example code and guides on using the OpenAI API, accessible at cookbook.openai.com

entries.q: What do I need to run OpenAI API examples
entries.a: To run OpenAI API examples, you need an OpenAI account and an associated API key

entries.q: How can I store my OpenAI API key for use in code examples
entries.a: You can store your OpenAI API key as an environment variable called OPENAI_API_KEY or in an .env file at the root of your repository

entries.q: What programming language are most OpenAI API code examples written in
entries.a: Most OpenAI API code examples are written in Python, but the concepts can be applied in any language

entries.q: Where can I find additional resources for learning about OpenAI and the API
entries.a: Additional resources for learning about OpenAI and the API can be found on the OpenAI Cookbook website, under related resources

entries.q: What license applies to the OpenAI Cookbook
entries.a: The OpenAI Cookbook is licensed under the MIT License

entries.q: How do I create a free OpenAI account to get started with the API
entries.a: You can create a free OpenAI account by signing up at platform.openai.com/signup
